captured_by: manual via Supabase MCP (env vars not available locally for coverage-report.mjs)
context: Baseline taken AFTER coverage-fix code shipped (commit b2f79b7) but the code has only had partial scrape cycles since. Treat as 'shortly-after-deploy' state, not pure pre-deploy. Compare against future runs to measure ongoing drift.

retry_queue.alert: All 200 exhausted rows are uprn lookups, 196 of which exhausted via circuit_open. The OS Places circuit breaker tripped during a hygiene-wave drain and the queue burned 5 attempts × 196 rows without doing useful work. The drain should skip retries when osBreaker.isOpen() rather than counting them as failed attempts. Filed as follow-up.

os_places_cache.note: Zero negative cache entries means either no recent OS Places no_match returns, or the negative-cache write path hasn't executed yet (recently deployed). Recheck after the next full hygiene wave.

observations.image_coverage_already_high: Aggregate 96.5% — the COVERAGE_FIX_PLAN's '~80-90%' estimate was off. Real outliers: Charles Darrow (17.6%), landwood (0%). Fix #1's value is concentrated on those two houses, not the whole pipeline.
observations.price_coverage_already_high: 97.5% aggregate. Plan's '70-85%' estimate was off. Edward Mellor (79.2%) is the only top-volume house with a meaningful gap.
observations.uprn_is_the_real_gap: 13.3% aggregate. Most houses sit at 0% — the OS Places API isn't being run on most houses (or runs are failing). Even Savills (best non-firstforauctions house at 93%) leaves room. Fix #2's UPRN re-targeting + retry queue is the highest-leverage fix in the entire plan.
observations.epc_is_an_underrated_gap: 36% aggregate, not even mentioned as a problem in COVERAGE_FIX_PLAN.md. Savills and Auction House National are at 50-72% but most others are <30%. Worth a follow-up plan.
observations.postcode_concentration: harmanhealy (38.7%), edwardmellor (1.9%), futureauctions (0%), cliveemson (0%) — postcode-less houses dominate the missing rows. Fix #2's gap-filler (Pass 2 in runEnrichmentWave) should be sweeping these.

follow_ups_uncovered_by_baseline: Retry-queue drain needs to check osBreaker.isOpen() before attempting — currently burns 5 attempts per row when circuit is tripped.
Charles Darrow + landwood image extractors are broken (0-17% coverage on a pipeline that averages 100%). Investigate as part of self-healing harness, not fix-#1's general lift.
Edward Mellor is missing postcodes (1.9%) — likely an extractor bug not addressed by COVERAGE_FIX_PLAN.
Negative cache hasn't filled yet — recheck after the next 24h cycle to confirm the new write path is running.

field_sources_non_empty exactly matches uprn count (1374) — provenance stamping currently only runs through the OS Places path. Fix #6's dense provenance via stampSourceIfEmpty in lib/pipeline/extractor.js should bring this much higher on the next scrape cycle.
